name: FinTrack
description: Full-stack personal finance tracker using Spring Boot APIs and a React UI for daily money management.
highlights: Built backend APIs for transaction flows and account-level data handling.
Implemented JWT-based authentication with user-scoped financial data access.
Added analytics-friendly dashboard views for spending and income trends.

name: NRV-Blog
description: Blog platform with React frontend, Spring Boot backend, and Spring Security-based access control.
highlights: Separated frontend and API layers for cleaner deployment and ownership.
Implemented secured authoring and content endpoints.
Kept the architecture straightforward for easy feature extension.

name: Spring-Microservices
description: Hands-on microservices sandbox covering Spring Cloud patterns, service decomposition, and cloud-native workflows.
highlights: Explored API gateway and centralized configuration flows.
Worked with service-to-service communication and modular boundaries.
Used container-first thinking for local and cloud-friendly execution.

name: Spring-Security
description: Spring Security playground for implementing practical auth and authorization flows.
highlights: Focused on role-based endpoint protection patterns.
Explored secure request handling in Java backend services.
Captured reusable security setup patterns for future projects.

name: Hotel-Microservice
description: Java microservice project centered on service boundaries and clean API design.
highlights: Worked on modular backend service structure.
Applied microservice communication and data-flow concepts.
Prioritized readable service contracts and maintainable code.

name: Spring-GraphQL
description: Demo project integrating Spring Boot with GraphQL for flexible query-driven APIs.
highlights: Set up GraphQL schema and resolver flow in Spring.
Compared query-based APIs with typical REST patterns.
Focused on clean mapping from service layer to GraphQL response.

name: LeetCodeSol
description: Cross-language problem-solving repository for algorithms and data structures.
highlights: Solutions maintained across C++, Java, Python, and TypeScript.
Used as a consistent practice base for DSA and coding discipline.
Helps sharpen thinking for edge cases and optimization trade-offs.
